performance: JPM: strongest quality profile, but shares are slightly softer over the last week.
BAC: earnings were solid, yet price action remains weak relative to the beat.
C: good earnings/guidance, but the market still treats it as a turnaround story.
WFC: cheapest among the large retail/commercial peers, but revenue softness caps enthusiasm.
GS: solid weekly resilience with a strong earnings/guidance backdrop.
MS: best recent momentum, backed by a clean beat and growth in wealth/capital markets.
key_drivers: Earnings beats across most of the group.
Guidance/capital return was constructive for C and GS.
Macro/rates remain the main cross-current for NII and deposit behavior in JPM/BAC/C/WFC.
Credit quality and commercial real estate remain the key downside risk for retail/commercial banks.
Capital markets and advisory/trading volatility matter most for GS and MS.
notable_risks: Net interest income compression if rates fall faster than deposit costs reset.
Credit normalization, especially consumer and CRE exposure.
Regulatory/capital requirements, particularly for the large money-center banks.
Trading and IB volatility for GS/MS if deal flow or market volumes slow.
Valuation risk for JPM/MS/GS if earnings momentum moderates.

summary.overall_readout: JPM remains fundamentally strong with solid ROE/ROA, moderate valuation, and constructive Q1 2026 results. Near-term price action softened over 1-3 months, while insider activity was predominantly senior-executive selling around earnings.
